title: cyathea gazellae
intro: Cyathea gazellae, also known as the Gazelle's fern, is a species of tree fern native to the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its rich content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cyathea gazellae include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a sought-after remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion, with some practitioners also using it in the form of tinctures or essential oils.
